III. 綜合測驗 說明:以下兩篇短文,共有 10 個空格,為第 21 至 30 題,每題有四個選項,請依各篇短文文意, 選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 ▲ 下篇短文共有 5 個空格,為第 21-25 題,請依短文文意,選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 A stopover in Seoul can be turned into an unforgettable adventure. Seoul boasts 21 unique cultural and historic landmarks. Travelers can engage themselves in Korean history by visiting palaces such as Gyeongbokgung and Deoksugung, 22 they can wander through the grand halls and admire the beautiful gardens. The changing of the guard ceremony is a spectacle that has attracted global attention. For food lovers, bustling markets and street stalls like Myeongdong and Namdaemun 23 delicious bibimbap, tteokbokki, and Korean barbecue. Modern attractions include COEX Mall, trendy cafés and restaurants. A trip 24 Namsan Tower gives a stunning 360-degree view of the city, and a jjimjilbang Korean sauna provides a chance to relax. Seoul presents endless opportunities to find something new, 25 unforgettable memories that will last a lifetime. Seoul has the possibility to become a remarkable experience.

It stimulates the immune system to recognize and fight disease-causing pathogens more effectively. Despite overwhelming scientific evidence that being fully vaccinated can prevent viral infections such as COVID-19 infection, the anti-vaccination movement appears to remain a controversial issue. Even so, most people still believe that getting vaccinated is a safe and effective way to prevent the spread of diseases and has greatly contributed to public health improvements worldwide. What’s more, vaccines are usually available for free or at an affordable cost. In order to avoid the embarrassing situation (to be or not to be vaccinated), Campbells Bay School has created a table to help students and parents understand the vaccination requirements for various school activities.

This insurance is vital for vehicle owners as it safeguards against unexpected circumstances and gives financial support for repairs, medical expenses, and third-party liabilities. There are two major types of motor insurance: third-party liability insurance and comprehensive insurance. Third-party liability insurance is required in many countries or areas, and it serves as a basic form of coverage, protecting third-party individuals from loss and harm. Comprehensive insurance, on the other hand, provides a higher degree of protection and covers damages to both parties involved in an accident, including the policyholder. Premiums for motor insurance vary based on several factors. These include the driver’s age and driving record, the type and age of the insured vehicle, and the desired level of insurance. Drivers with a positive driving history or those who install safety features in their vehicles may qualify for lower premiums. Motor insurance can be applied to all types of motor vehicles, such as cars, motorcycles, jeeps, and commercial vehicles. However, many countries, such as India, have witnessed a persistent occurrence of accidents involving cars, motorcycles, and scooters. As a result, in many countries, emphasis is placed on third-party liabilities for these vehicles. The government of India, for example, has made premium adjustments to ensure preparation for potential risks. Further information can be found in the accompanying chart. Insurance Premiums The government has announced an increase in the third-party motor insurance premium for various categories from June 1.

There are 3 types of two-wheelers insurance premium and 4 types of car insurance. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 38-42 題 Hunger marketing is a marketing technique that creates a sense of urgency and exclusivity around a product or service. This technique often limits the supply of a product or service to create a sense of scarcity, which encourages customers to act quickly to secure the product or service. The intention is to build up a buzz around the product, and often, companies will use social media platforms and influencer marketing to help spread the word. Several notable examples of hunger marketing are worth mentioning. In 2011, Taiwan witnessed the frenzy of Yi-Mei Thick Milk Tea. During that time, the product gained a significant amount of attention and popularity among consumers. Another example is the famous 2015 marketing campaign of Nestle’s KitKat. They presented 500 limited edition gold chocolate bars distributed to seven countries and prompted consumers to hunt down the bars in a treasure hunt across the globe. Hunger marketing makes good use of human psychological weakness that people desire things more when they are scarce. This shortage can generate a sense of urgency and the fear of missing out, motivating consumers to make a purchase. However, there are also some disadvantages to this approach. For example, if a company overestimates the demand for their products, they may be left with excess stock, which can be costly to handle. It can also lead to customers feeling angered or misled if they are not able to purchase the product they desire. In a nutshell, hunger marketing can be an effective marketing strategy for businesses to promote their products or services and make demand. It is still essential to use this technique with caution and carefully plan to ensure that it does not backfire.
